message:
Add btrfs RW fallocate capability contract and E2E smoke harness

The FUSE core library gains ~249 lines of new functionality for btrfs
read-write path handling including fallocate support with capability
contract enforcement. The E2E test suite gets a comprehensive 347-line
btrfs RW smoke test that validates mount, write, fallocate, and
unmount sequences under both strict and lenient modes.

- ffs-core/src/lib.rs: btrfs fallocate RW capability contract with
  mode-aware enforcement and error propagation
- scripts/e2e/ffs_btrfs_rw_smoke.sh: full lifecycle smoke test for
  btrfs RW operations (mount, write, fallocate, fsync, unmount)
- FEATURE_PARITY.md: updated parity tracking for new btrfs RW items
- .beads/issues.jsonl: issue tracker sync
- scripts/e2e/README.md: E2E test index updated

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-h6nz","title":"EPIC: V1.1 Post-Parity Hardening and Operationalization","description":"# EPIC: V1.1 Post-Parity Hardening and Operationalization

## Context
Tracked V1 parity is complete (75/75), but FrankenFS is explicitly still early-stage and experimental for mount/write operations. The project now needs a rigorous post-parity program focused on production-grade correctness confidence, failure containment, observability, and predictable performance under stress.

This epic converts that post-parity intent into an executable dependency graph that is:
- granular enough for independent agent execution,
- explicit about acceptance gates,
- self-contained (no need to reopen planning docs during execution).

## Why this exists
Parity completion proves breadth of implemented behavior, not necessarily depth of operational hardening. Remaining risk lives in:
- durability under crash/fault scenarios,
- clarity of experimental behavior (especially btrfs RW path),
- incomplete operational wiring (CLI mount path for managed backpressure/per-core hooks),
- benchmark/regression signal fidelity,
- unresolved spec open questions that can cause architecture drift if left ambiguous.

## Scope
This epic is fulfilled when all child epics below are complete:
1. Durable MVCC version-store implementation and replay hardening.
2. CLI mount wiring for managed backpressure/per-core scheduling.
3. btrfs experimental RW hardening and explicit compatibility contracts.
4. Fuzzing and adversarial test-depth expansion.
5. Benchmark fidelity and regression-guard upgrades.
6. Closure of formal open questions (with accepted decisions integrated into docs/tests/code).

## Non-goals
- Reopening excluded V1 scope items (multi-device btrfs, fscrypt, inline_data, etc.) unless explicitly approved in a separate scope-change epic.
- Marketing/docs polish without technical acceptance deltas.

## Definition of Done
- Child epics closed with evidence-backed acceptance criteria.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md, spec docs, and harness signals remain internally consistent.
- New work items include explicit test and observability requirements, not implementation-only checklists.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:48:35.532955522Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:04:12.361294363Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_program_root"],"comments":[{"id":294,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Execution sequencing recommendation (to avoid coordination deadlocks):

2) In parallel, advance implementation-ready tracks that already have clear contracts (`bd-h6nz.2.*`, `bd-h6nz.4.*`, `bd-h6nz.5.*`).
3) Begin durable MVCC implementation (`bd-h6nz.1.*`) once OQ7/OQ4 decisions are available where required.
4) Use `bd-h6nz.7.*` after telemetry and behavior contracts are stable.

Reasoning:
- This order minimizes rework from policy churn.
- It keeps high-throughput execution by allowing independent tracks to proceed in parallel.
- It prevents communication purgatory by giving every agent a concrete lane with clear dependencies.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:55:07Z"},{"id":347,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space optimization note (2026-03-03):\n- Removed over-broad cross-epic blockers (bd-h6nz.3<-bd-h6nz.6.3, bd-h6nz.5<-bd-h6nz.2, bd-h6nz.7<-{bd-h6nz.1,bd-h6nz.2,bd-h6nz.3}) to increase safe parallelism and avoid blocking noise.\n- Kept correctness constraints at task-level where they are actually needed (e.g., bd-h6nz.3.4 still depends on bd-h6nz.6.3).\n- Strengthened benchmark gate coupling with mount-mode gate (bd-h6nz.5.6 depends on bd-h6nz.2.6).\n- Added telemetry dependency for degraded-runtime benchmarking (bd-h6nz.5.4 depends on bd-h6nz.2.4).\nRationale: maximize throughput in plan space without reducing scope or lowering acceptance quality.","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:04:12Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1","title":"EPIC: Durable MVCC Version Store and Crash Replay","description":"# EPIC: Durable MVCC Version Store and Crash Replay

## Problem Statement
MVCC currently provides strong in-memory isolation and conflict semantics, but the long-term architecture calls for a durable version-store path with restart replay guarantees. This is explicitly documented as planned in the spec and is a key reliability boundary between "experimental" and "operationally trustworthy" write paths.

## Why this matters
Without a durable MVCC version-store:
- commit visibility across process restart is less explicit,
- crash-time reconstruction relies on narrower recovery channels,
- validation of full write-path durability semantics remains incomplete.

## Scope
Implement and harden a persistent MVCC version-store workflow including:
1. append protocol and on-disk record format,
2. replay engine and corruption handling,
3. mount/open integration + observability,
4. deterministic crash matrix coverage.

## Required Evidence
- deterministic replay tests proving latest committed state reconstruction,
- corrupted/truncated log handling tests with explicit outcomes,
- metrics and CLI visibility for replay/apply/skip decisions.

## Done Criteria
- Child tasks completed with green gates and replay evidence.
- Spec/architecture docs updated from "planned" to implemented behavior.
- No silent recovery ambiguity (each replay path emits a reasoned outcome).","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:48:43.695973833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:59:55.756410424Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:48:43.695973833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":295,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note:
Treat this epic as a correctness program, not a feature program. Every step should produce replay-oracle evidence. If a design choice cannot be tested deterministically under crash schedules, it is not ready to ship.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:55:07Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","title":"Define durable MVCC version-store record format and invariants","description":"## Objective
Turn the planned durable MVCC record model into an implementable contract with no ambiguity around ordering, checksums, atomicity boundaries, and forward compatibility.

## Background
Spec section 5.9 defines high-level goals. This task crystallizes the concrete wire/storage format and invariant ledger required before implementation.

## Deliverables
1. Record schema for version-store entries (header + payload + integrity fields).
2. Explicit ordering model (commit_seq monotonicity, tie-breaking, duplicate handling).
3. Crash consistency points (what is guaranteed after power loss at each append stage).
4. Corruption semantics (truncate vs reject vs quarantine behavior).
5. Compatibility/versioning strategy for future schema evolution.

## Required Artifacts
- Spec updates in COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md + PROPOSED_ARCHITECTURE.md.
- Invariant checklist suitable for test oracles.
- Example binary layout snippets and decode walkthrough.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No undecided fields in the record layout.
- Replay behavior is fully deterministic for all malformed/torn cases.
- At least 5 explicit invariants are documented and test-mappable.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract
1. Unit Tests (required)
- Add or extend crate-local tests for happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit error paths.
- Include at least one invariant-focused negative test proving failure handling is intentional.

2. Deterministic E2E Scripts (required)
- Add or extend deterministic E2E coverage (prefer `scripts/e2e/` or existing harness E2E path) with explicit setup/teardown.
- Every scenario must emit machine-parseable pass/fail markers plus stable `scenario_id` values.

3. Structured Logging (required)
- Emit structured logs at key state transitions and failure boundaries.
- Critical events must include `operation_id`, `scenario_id`, and outcome/error classification fields.
- Add test assertions (unit/integration/E2E) validating critical log markers for both success and failure paths.

4. Validation Evidence (required)
- Closure must include artifact paths and concise evidence summary for tests + logs.
- CPU-intensive gates must be run via `rch exec -- ...` when invoking cargo builds/checks/tests/benches.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:34.532430678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:06:07.431205340Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_mvcc_durable","qa_e2e_required","qa_structured_logging_required","qa_unit_required"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:34.532430678Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":305,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.1.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Plan-space revision (quality contract, mandatory before closure):

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1.2","title":"Implement append-only durable MVCC log writer with integrity checks","description":"## Objective
Implement the write path for durable version-store records in `ffs-mvcc`, including checksum emission, append atomicity discipline, and bounded fsync strategy.

## Scope
1. Writer API for staging + append + durability boundary.
2. Per-record integrity fields and verification-on-write.
3. Batching policy with explicit tradeoffs (latency vs durability).
4. Backpressure integration when persistence path saturates.

## Guardrails
- No silent drop of records on partial I/O.
- Distinguish retryable I/O errors vs fatal format/invariant violations.
- Emit structured logs for append lifecycle and failure classes.

## Tests
- Unit tests for normal append, short write, torn write simulation, checksum mismatch.
- Property test for monotonic ordering preservation across randomized append sequences.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Appended records decode correctly by a strict reader.
- Failure cases return explicit typed errors (not generic format strings).
- `cargo test -p ffs-mvcc` includes deterministic coverage for append failure modes.

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1.3","title":"Implement durable MVCC replay engine with corruption/torn-record recovery policy","description":"## Objective
Build restart replay for durable version-store records, producing deterministic reconstruction of latest committed visibility while handling partial/corrupt tails safely.

## Scope
1. Replay scanner and decoder with strict validation.
2. Tail-handling policy (truncate-to-last-good vs fail-fast with operator override).
3. Replay outcome classification (`clean`, `truncated_tail`, `corrupt_abort`, etc.).
4. Integration into MVCC in-memory structures without violating snapshot invariants.

## Tests
- Replay from clean log.
- Replay with truncated final record.
- Replay with mid-log corruption.
- Replay idempotence (multiple replays yield identical state).
- Replay ordering correctness across randomized commit sequences.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Replay outcomes are explicit and auditable.
- Invariant checks after replay pass for all supported scenarios.
- Corruption handling never produces silent partial visibility.
## Mandatory Test & Logging Contract

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.1.4","title":"Integrate durable MVCC store into OpenFs lifecycle and mount options","description":"## Objective
Wire durable MVCC persistence and replay into `OpenFs::open` / mount lifecycle with explicit mode controls and safe defaults.

## Scope
1. Open-time replay invocation and replay summary capture.
2. Mode flags for strict vs tolerant replay handling.
3. Read-only fallback behavior when replay policy requires it.
4. Integration with existing journal/recovery reporting surfaces.

## Constraints
- No hidden behavior changes for default read-only mount semantics.
- Replay failure modes must map to actionable CLI errors.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Mount/open path clearly reports replay decisions.
- Recovery + replay ordering is deterministic and documented.
- Integration tests cover clean start, crash restart, and replay-failure paths.

 {"id":"bd-h6nz.4","title":"EPIC: Fuzzing and Adversarial Input Expansion","description":"# EPIC: Fuzzing and Adversarial Input Expansion

## Problem Statement
The repo has a useful `tests/fuzz_corpus/` seed set, but a comprehensive fuzzing lifecycle requires first-class targets, repeatable automation, and crash-to-regression promotion workflows.

## Why this matters
Metadata parsers and recovery logic are high-risk surfaces where bug classes are often discovered by generated adversarial inputs rather than hand-authored unit tests.

## Scope
1. Add/maintain dedicated fuzz targets for critical parser and mutation surfaces.
2. Reuse and grow the existing corpus in a structured way.
3. Automate nightly fuzz runs and triage artifacts.
4. Guarantee reproducibility and promotion of minimized crashers into deterministic tests.

## Done Criteria
- Fuzz targets exist for priority surfaces and run in CI/nightly policy.
- Crasher minimization/promotion path is documented and exercised.
- Corpus growth is intentional (coverage-guided), not accidental file accumulation.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:05.478828048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:00:00.011911193Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lane_fuzzing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-h6nz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:05.478828048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":298,"issue_id":"bd-h6nz.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Future-self note:

+wait_for_pid_exit() {
+    local pid="$1"
+    local timeout_seconds="${2:-10}"
+    local label="${3:-process}"
+    local elapsed_ticks=0
+
+    while kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null; do
+        sleep 0.2
+        elapsed_ticks=$((elapsed_ticks + 1))
+        if [[ $elapsed_ticks -ge $((timeout_seconds * 5)) ]]; then
+            e2e_log "$label did not exit after ${timeout_seconds}s; sending SIGKILL"
+            kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
+            break
+        fi
+    done
+
+    wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
+}

+run_crash_matrix_rw_steps() {
+    local mount_point="$1"
+    local point="$2"
+    python3 - "$mount_point" "$point" <<'PY'
+import os
+import pathlib
+import sys
+
+mount = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1])
+point = int(sys.argv[2])
+if point < 1 or point > 10:
+    raise SystemExit(f"unsupported crash matrix point: {point}")
+
+matrix = mount / "crash_matrix"
+alpha = matrix / "alpha.txt"
+beta = matrix / "beta.txt"
+gamma = matrix / "gamma.txt"
+alpha_v1 = b"alpha-v1\n"
+alpha_v2 = b"alpha-v2\n"
+gamma_seed = b"gamma-seed\n"
+
+matrix.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+
+def fsync_file(path: pathlib.Path) -> None:
+    fd = os.open(path, os.O_RDONLY)
+    try:
+        os.fsync(fd)
+    finally:
+        os.close(fd)
+
+def fsync_dir(path: pathlib.Path) -> None:
+    fd = os.open(str(path), os.O_RDONLY)
+    try:
+        os.fsync(fd)
+    finally:
+        os.close(fd)
+
+def step_1_create_alpha_no_fsync() -> None:
+    alpha.write_bytes(alpha_v1)
+
+def step_2_append_alpha_no_fsync() -> None:
+    with alpha.open("ab") as handle:
+        handle.write(alpha_v2)
+
+def step_3_fsync_alpha_and_parent() -> None:
+    fsync_file(alpha)
+    fsync_dir(matrix)
+
+def step_4_rename_alpha_to_beta_no_fsync() -> None:
+    alpha.rename(beta)
+
+def step_5_fsync_rename_parent() -> None:
+    fsync_dir(matrix)
+
+def step_6_create_gamma_no_fsync() -> None:
+    gamma.write_bytes(gamma_seed)
+
+def step_7_fsync_gamma_and_parent() -> None:
+    fsync_file(gamma)
+    fsync_dir(matrix)
+
+def step_8_unlink_beta_no_fsync() -> None:
+    if beta.exists():
+        beta.unlink()
+
+def step_9_unlink_gamma_no_fsync() -> None:
+    if gamma.exists():
+        gamma.unlink()
+
+def step_10_fsync_unlink_parent() -> None:
+    fsync_dir(matrix)
+
+steps = [
+    step_1_create_alpha_no_fsync,
+    step_2_append_alpha_no_fsync,
+    step_3_fsync_alpha_and_parent,
+    step_4_rename_alpha_to_beta_no_fsync,
+    step_5_fsync_rename_parent,
+    step_6_create_gamma_no_fsync,
+    step_7_fsync_gamma_and_parent,
+    step_8_unlink_beta_no_fsync,
+    step_9_unlink_gamma_no_fsync,
+    step_10_fsync_unlink_parent,
+]
+
+for idx, step in enumerate(steps, start=1):
+    step()
+    if idx == point:
+        break
+PY
+}

+verify_crash_matrix_ro_state() {
+    local mount_point="$1"
+    local point="$2"
+    local report_path="$3"
+    python3 - "$mount_point" "$point" "$report_path" <<'PY'
+import json
+import pathlib
+import sys
+
+mount = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[1])
+point = int(sys.argv[2])
+report_path = pathlib.Path(sys.argv[3])
+
+matrix = mount / "crash_matrix"
+alpha = matrix / "alpha.txt"
+beta = matrix / "beta.txt"
+gamma = matrix / "gamma.txt"
+alpha_full = b"alpha-v1\nalpha-v2\n"
+gamma_full = b"gamma-seed\n"
+errors = []
+
+def read_bytes(path: pathlib.Path):
+    if path.exists():
+        return path.read_bytes()
+    return None
+
+def prefix_ok(value, expected):
+    if value is None:
+        return True
+    return expected.startswith(value)
+
+alpha_data = read_bytes(alpha)
+beta_data = read_bytes(beta)
+gamma_data = read_bytes(gamma)
+
+if point in (1, 2):
+    if beta_data is not None:
+        errors.append("beta.txt must not exist before rename points")
+    if gamma_data is not None:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt must not exist before gamma-create points")
+    if alpha_data is not None and not prefix_ok(alpha_data, alpha_full):
+        errors.append("alpha.txt contains unexpected bytes before fsync boundary")
+elif point == 3:
+    if alpha_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt must persist full payload after fsync boundary")
+    if beta_data is not None:
+        errors.append("beta.txt must not exist at crash point 3")
+    if gamma_data is not None:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt must not exist at crash point 3")
+elif point == 4:
+    if gamma_data is not None:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt must not exist at crash point 4")
+    if alpha_data is None and beta_data is None:
+        errors.append("expected alpha.txt or beta.txt after rename without fsync")
+    if alpha_data is not None and alpha_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt (if present) must match full payload at crash point 4")
+    if beta_data is not None and beta_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("beta.txt (if present) must match full payload at crash point 4")
+elif point == 5:
+    if alpha_data is not None:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt must be absent after fsync rename boundary")
+    if beta_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("beta.txt must persist full payload after fsync rename boundary")
+    if gamma_data is not None:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt must not exist at crash point 5")
+elif point == 6:
+    if alpha_data is not None:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt must be absent at crash point 6")
+    if beta_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("beta.txt must persist full payload at crash point 6")
+    if gamma_data is not None and not prefix_ok(gamma_data, gamma_full):
+        errors.append("gamma.txt contains unexpected bytes before fsync boundary")
+elif point == 7:
+    if alpha_data is not None:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt must be absent at crash point 7")
+    if beta_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("beta.txt must persist full payload at crash point 7")
+    if gamma_data != gamma_full:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt must persist full payload after fsync boundary")
+elif point == 8:
+    if alpha_data is not None:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt must be absent at crash point 8")
+    if beta_data is not None and beta_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("beta.txt (if present) must match full payload at crash point 8")
+    if gamma_data != gamma_full:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt must persist full payload at crash point 8")
+elif point == 9:
+    if alpha_data is not None:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt must be absent at crash point 9")
+    if beta_data is not None and beta_data != alpha_full:
+        errors.append("beta.txt (if present) must match full payload at crash point 9")
+    if gamma_data is not None and gamma_data != gamma_full:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt (if present) must match full payload at crash point 9")
+elif point == 10:
+    if alpha_data is not None:
+        errors.append("alpha.txt must be absent after unlink fsync boundary")
+    if beta_data is not None:
+        errors.append("beta.txt must be absent after unlink fsync boundary")
+    if gamma_data is not None:
+        errors.append("gamma.txt must be absent after unlink fsync boundary")
+else:
+    errors.append(f"unsupported crash matrix point: {point}")
+
+if matrix.exists():
+    for child in matrix.iterdir():
+        if child.is_file():
+            try:
+                _ = child.read_bytes()
+            except OSError as exc:
+                errors.append(f"failed to read file during consistency walk: {child.name}: {exc}")
+
+report = {
+    "crash_point": point,
+    "alpha_present": alpha_data is not None,
+    "beta_present": beta_data is not None,
+    "gamma_present": gamma_data is not None,
+    "alpha_size": None if alpha_data is None else len(alpha_data),
+    "beta_size": None if beta_data is None else len(beta_data),
+    "gamma_size": None if gamma_data is None else len(gamma_data),
+    "errors": errors,
+}
+report_path.write_text(json.dumps(report, indent=2, sort_keys=True), encoding="utf-8")
+if errors:
+    for entry in errors:
+        print(entry)
+    raise SystemExit(1)
+PY
+}

+run_crash_matrix_point() {
+    local point="$1"
+    local label="$2"
+    local point_id
+    point_id="$(printf '%02d' "$point")"
+
+    local operation_id="btrfs-crash-matrix-point-${point_id}"
+    local scenario_id="btrfs_rw_crash_matrix_${point_id}_${label}"
+    local scenario_image="$E2E_TEMP_DIR/crash_matrix_${point_id}.btrfs"
+    local mount_rw_point="$E2E_TEMP_DIR/mnt_crash_rw_${point_id}"
+    local mount_ro_point="$E2E_TEMP_DIR/mnt_crash_ro_${point_id}"
+    local artifact_dir="$E2E_LOG_DIR/crash_matrix/${point_id}_${label}"
+    local report_path="$artifact_dir/recovery_verify_report.json"
+    mkdir -p "$artifact_dir"
+
+    cp "$BASE_IMAGE" "$scenario_image"
+    crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "start" "none"
+
+    start_mount rw "$scenario_image" "$mount_rw_point" 20
+    local rw_mount_log="$CURRENT_MOUNT_LOG"
+
+    if ! run_crash_matrix_rw_steps "$mount_rw_point" "$point"; then
+        crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "rejected" "rw_step_execution_failed"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ "$point" == "3" || "$point" == "5" || "$point" == "7" || "$point" == "10" ]]; then
+        if ! grep -q "btrfs_sync_applied" "$rw_mount_log"; then
+            crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "rejected" "missing_sync_applied_log"
+            return 1
+        fi
+        if ! grep -q "operation_id=btrfs-fsync" "$rw_mount_log" && ! grep -q "operation_id=btrfs-fsyncdir" "$rw_mount_log"; then
+            crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "rejected" "missing_sync_operation_id_log"
+            return 1
+        fi
+        if ! grep -q 'scenario_id="btrfs_rw_fsync' "$rw_mount_log"; then
+            crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "rejected" "missing_sync_scenario_id_log"
+            return 1
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "crash_injected" "none"
+    if [[ -n "$CURRENT_MOUNT_PID" ]] && kill -0 "$CURRENT_MOUNT_PID" 2>/dev/null; then
+        kill -9 "$CURRENT_MOUNT_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
+        wait_for_pid_exit "$CURRENT_MOUNT_PID" 10 "crash-matrix mount daemon"
+    fi
+    CURRENT_MOUNT_PID=""
+    e2e_unmount "$mount_rw_point" || true
+    cp "$rw_mount_log" "$artifact_dir/mount_rw.log" || true
+
+    if ! "$FFS_CLI_BIN" inspect "$scenario_image" --json >"$artifact_dir/inspect_after_crash.json" 2>"$artifact_dir/inspect_after_crash.stderr"; then
+        crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "rejected" "post_crash_inspect_failed"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    start_mount ro "$scenario_image" "$mount_ro_point" 20
+    local ro_mount_log="$CURRENT_MOUNT_LOG"
+
+    if ! verify_crash_matrix_ro_state "$mount_ro_point" "$point" "$report_path"; then
+        crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "rejected" "recovery_verification_failed"
+        cp "$ro_mount_log" "$artifact_dir/mount_ro.log" || true
+        stop_mount "$mount_ro_point"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    cp "$ro_mount_log" "$artifact_dir/mount_ro.log" || true
+    stop_mount "$mount_ro_point"
+    crash_matrix_event_log "$operation_id" "$scenario_id" "applied" "none"
+    return 0
+}
